feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: Walker Buehler never imagined returning to Dodger Stadium like this
entry.content: Walker Buehler returns to Chavez Ravine in the most unlikely jersey.

entry.title: Timberwolves' Anthony Edwards has no more excuses after LaMelo Ball blockbuster
entry.content: Anthony Edwards facing extra pressure with Timberwolves' LaMelo Ball trade.

entry.title: Where is Didier Deschamps? Explaining France coach's absence from World Cup game vs. Norway
entry.content: France will be without their coach for their final World Cup group game against Norway.

entry.title: Mark Tame obituary
entry.content: My friend Mark Tame, who has died of lung cancer aged 61, spent his life caring for others. He worked for various charities (including Mind) in Bristol and London that advocated for mental health patients, and spent the past five years at Homerton healthcare NHS foundation trust in London as a care coordinator after the Covid-19 pandemic.Mark was born in Wivenhoe, Essex, the son of Anne Tame, a telephonist, and John Matthews, a telephone engineer, who met in the postwar period at Bletchley Park, the codebreaking centre in Buckinghamshire. He went to several different schools, then Bristol University, where he studied social politics, which became an anchor of not only his career, but of his personal ethics. Continue reading...

entry.title: Wimbledon 2026 live draw: Updated bracket, seedings for Serena Williams and more in singles, doubles
entry.content: The Sporting News is tracking live results from Friday's draw, including where Williams will start her Wimbledon journey in singles and doubles.

entry.title: What channel is the NHL Draft on today? Start time, TV schedule, live stream, picks order for 2026 coverage
entry.content: Here's everything you need to know about the 2026 NHL Draft, including TV channel and streaming options for each round.

entry.title: What channel is Padres vs. Dodgers on today? Time, TV schedule, live stream to watch MLB Friday baseball game
entry.content: Division rivals meet for their second series of the season at Petco Park. Here's when and where to watch the opener.

entry.title: Uruguay vs. Spain projected lineups, starting 11 for World Cup 2026 Group H game in Guadalajara
entry.content: Uruguay and Spain meet on Friday at Estadio Akron in Guadalajara in a World Cup Group H game with very different stakes for each side.

entry.title: California voters will consider a measure in November to raise taxes on billionaires
entry.content: Californians will vote in November on a controversial proposal to impose a one-time tax on billionaires to help fund federal funding cuts to healthcare for low-income people

entry.title: Texas school board to vote on required Bible readings in public education
entry.content: The Texas board of education is set to vote on a required reading list for more than 5 million public school students that includes stories and passages from the Bible

entry.title: Pochettino annoyed by perceived disappointment after US finishes group play with 3-2 loss to Turkey
entry.content: Kaan Ayhan scored on the final kick of the match, and Turkey beat the United States 3-2 for its only win of the World Cup

entry.title: 74-year-old man convicted of killing wife becomes the oldest inmate executed in modern Florida history
entry.content: 74-year-old man convicted of killing wife becomes the oldest inmate executed in modern Florida history

entry.title: Rent board fulfills Mamdani's vow to freeze the rent on 1 million NYC apartments
entry.content: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani promised a rent freeze for people living in 1 million apartments if he got elected

entry.title: The Mission review – a surgeon saves lives in war-torn Gaza in a visceral portrait of human endurance
entry.content: Mohammad Tahir and his colleagues operate through bombing and blackouts in barely functional hospitals – but there are moments of relief amid the documentary’s tragedy and goreWhat this documentary might lack in film-making finesse it makes up for with sheer visceral and emotional impact. British nerve surgeon Mohammad Tahir and his colleagues, who also work the cameras, toil in Gaza’s barely operational hospitals during some of the worst days and nights of the war in the winter of 2024-25. Supported by US-based charity FAJR Global, who provide medical care to the world’s most in need, Tahir operates through bombings and blackouts with a bare minimum of medical supplies, sometimes treating patients lying on the floor in puddles of blood because there are no gurneys. This is often hard to watch, and not just because of all the gore; many of the victims are children, out of whom Tahir and the others dig bullets as well as tiny tungsten cubes, new-fangled shrapnel designed to cause maximum damage.With his matinee-star good looks, rock-steady composure and air of unruffled competence, Tahir makes an excellent guide to all this mayhem. For the most part he soldiers stoically on, but the cool melts when, for instance, he discusses how he had to remove a random jawbone embedded in a patient’s wound. Later on, he treats a little girl who has lost an arm in a bombing; he manages to reattach it after the family find the severed limb in the rubble of their home. The film could have easily started to feel like a numbing, endless procession of tragedy and bloodshed but the film-makers wisely offer a few moments of respite, such as a sequence where Tahir and his fellow medics enjoy a day out at the beach. Likewise, a scene where he teases a medical student bent over her textbooks briefly lightens the load of gloom. Continue reading...

entry.title: The Night the Ground Wouldn’t Stop Shaking in Venezuela
entry.content: Two powerful earthquakes marked the start of a terrifying night of aftershocks, testing the country’s U.S.-backed government.

entry.title: China Sharpens Tools for Hitting Back at Foreign Sanctions
entry.content: Beijing is preparing a new law that would add to its ability to punish foreign companies and individuals deemed to harm Chinese interests.

entry.title: A deadlocked jury in the Palisades Fire trial leaves attorneys weighing next steps
entry.content: A jury is deadlocked in the trial of Jonathan Rinderknecht, who is accused of starting the deadly 2025 Palisades Fire in Los Angeles

entry.title: Brothers are accused of mishandling remains of two dozen people at Colorado funeral home
entry.content: Police have arrested two Colorado men accused of mishandling the bodies of at least two dozen people

entry.title: Judge blocks Trump plan that would limit graduate student loans in nursing and other fields
entry.content: A federal judge has blocked part of a Trump administration plan that would have limited access to loans for students pursuing graduate degrees in nursing, physical therapy, public health and some other fields

entry.title: Ukraine’s Growing Drone Armada Is Overwhelming Russia’s Air Defenses
entry.content: Kyiv’s escalating campaign is leading to restrictions on fuel sales in Russia, surging gasoline prices and regular images of black smoke clouds.

entry.title: Judge orders release of more Epstein-files names that were redacted
entry.content: A federal judge in Washington on Thursday ordered the Justice Department to release information redacted or withheld from the public Epstein files, including names of people prosecutors once identified as "co-conspirators" and more details about an FBI interview with a woman who claimed President Trump assaulted her.

entry.title: How Iran Devastated an American Naval Base—and Caused a U.S. Recalculation
entry.content: Satellite imagery reveals for the first time the extent of what Iran destroyed at Naval Support Activity Bahrain.

entry.title: New York City Board Approves Mamdani’s Rent Freeze
entry.content: In its annual vote, the Rent Guidelines Board passed 0% rent increases for rent-regulated apartments.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
Drive like hell, you will get there.
Lucky Numbers: 22, 36, 75, 56, 56, 88

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Always write down momentarily good ideas. Don't rely on your memory alone.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: During the chariot scene in "Ben Hur," a small red car can be seen in the distance.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Voltaire
entry.content: “It is dangerous to be right in matters on which the established authorities are wrong.” — Voltaire

entry.title: The White House is asking OpenAI to slow roll the release of its new model over safety concerns
entry.content: penAI reportedly plans to share its newest model, GPT 5.6, with a select group of partners instead of to the broader public. The reason: the Trump administration told it to.

entry.title: Former youth pastor dies day he was set to appear in court for the death of his wife
entry.content: Former youth pastor dies day he was set to appear in court for the death of his wife

entry.title: Rent board poised to fulfill Mamdani's vow to freeze the rent on 1 million NYC apartments
entry.content: Rent board poised to fulfill Mamdani's vow to freeze the rent on 1 million NYC apartments

entry.title: YouTube Shorts are getting even shorter with an update that lets you double the playback speed
entry.content: YouTube Shorts is getting a makeover.

entry.title: Supreme Court Hands Trump Double Victory on Immigration Enforcement
entry.content: Plus, Iran’s ship attack in the Strait of Hormuz tests Trump’s deal, and English roadside diners are turning into sex shops.

entry.title: La Cruz offers a musical memorial to Pulse shooting victims on Pride Month
entry.content: Gay reggaeton MC La Cruz talks his sensual new EP, "El Nene, Vol. 2" — which features Brazilian pop star Gloria Groove and honors victims of the 2016 Pulse nightclub shooting.

entry.title: Jury reaches verdict in trial of Palisades Fire arson suspect
entry.content: A jury has reached a verdict in the case against Jonathan Rinderknecht, the man accused of starting the blaze that turned into the deadly and destructive Palisades Fire in 2025.

entry.title: Jury reaches a verdict in trial over deadly, destructive Palisades Fire
entry.content: The jury in the trial about the origin of the devastating January 2025 fires in Los Angeles has reached a verdict

entry.title: Fed's preferred inflation gauge hits highest level since 2023
entry.content: The Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge climbed to 4.1% in May, its highest level in more than three years. Josh Schafer, Barron's Investor Circle Newsletter editor, joins with analysis.

entry.title: IFRC official on efforts to help Venezuela earthquake victims
entry.content: Rescue efforts in Venezuela are underway after two powerful earthquakes struck the country on Wednesday night. Loyce Pace, International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies regional director for the Americas, joins CBS News to discuss the state of aid.

entry.title: With Caitlin Clark in the center of another officiating mess, it's past time for WNBA to evolve and meet the moment
entry.content: It’s time to be proactive and fix this entire officiating debacle, whether or not Caitlin Clark is at the center of it.

entry.title: San Antonio Spurs given A, partially due to drafting Jayden Quaintance
entry.content: The San Antonio Spurs drafted Jayden Quaintance with their first pick, and ESPN gave them one of the highest grades of any NBA team.

entry.title: “Here we go”: Man United target Elliot Anderson set to join Man City
entry.content: Manchester City and Nottingham Forest have reached an agreement over a deal for Manchester United midfield target Elliot Anderson.United interestHeading into the summer window, Anderson is believed to...

entry.title: Chicago Fed President Goolsbee says inflation is too high; Williams sees price pressures easing
entry.content: In a live CNBC interview from his home district, Goolsbee declined to speculate on where he thinks interest rates are headed.

entry.title: 🚨 BREAKING: Manchester City 'strike agreement' to sign Elliot Anderson
entry.content: Manchester City appear to have secured their man in midfield. The Premier League giants have been in negotiations with Nottingham Forest over Englishman Elliot Anderson, but according to David Ornstei...

entry.title: Former Tennessee basketball, Catholic guard BJ Edwards signs with Sacramento Kings
entry.content: Former Tennessee basketball and Knoxville Catholic guard BJ Edwards has signed a deal with the Sacramento Kings after going undrafted in the 2026 NBA Draft.

entry.title: India and South Africa take semi-final fight to final day
entry.content: The group stage of the Women's T20 World Cup concludes on Sunday with a must-win fixture for India against Australia at Lord's.

entry.title: All attention pointed at Fever’s Sophie Cunningham after viral moment
entry.content: If you had any doubts about Sophie Cunningham’s ability to captivate the social media universe, well, the finger is now pointed directly at you. Maybe most of the discourse in the WNBA this week revolves around her Indiana Fever teammate, Caitlin Clark, and a beef with referees about technical fouls and missed calls. But there was a hilariously saucy spin-off to Monday night’s dust-up involving Clark and Phoenix’s DeWanna Bonner: Cunningham once again asserting her place as the center of attenti

entry.title: Native Americans commemorate victory at Little Bighorn with horse races, dance and song
entry.content: The quiet, wind-swept hills of the Battle of Greasy Grass, known to many as the Battle of Little Bighorn, are the setting for Native Americans commemorating the battle's 150th anniversary with horse rides, battle reenactments and a camp of hundreds of people this week.

entry.title: Meme-Stock Traders Rally Around Wendy’s
entry.content: Shares of the beloved—but beleaguered—chain surged after individual investors rushed to defend ‘our pigtailed savior’ from detractors.

entry.title: A Democrat-on-Democrat Showdown Is Erupting Over Chicago’s Schools
entry.content: The battle between a billionaire and the teachers union mirrors wider power struggles within the party.

entry.title: K-pop singer Samuel honors his Latino roots with flair in new EP 'Samuelito'
entry.content: Born in L.A. to Korean and Mexican parents, K-pop idol Samuel explains why he fashioned his own "K-tone" fusion sound in his new EP, "Samuelito"

entry.title: Taylor Swift wedding venue speculation fueled by MSG permit
entry.content: There appear to be new clues about the location of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding.

entry.title: Former Las Vegas pastor dies in custody after being charged in wife's murder at Zion National Park
entry.content: A Las Vegas man charged with murdering his wife two decades ago at Zion National Park died in custody Thursday, just days after his arrest, before he could appear in court on the charges.

entry.title: Hundreds of veterans snag World Cup tickets
entry.content: A program for veterans, current military and first responders secured almost 5,000 free tickets for World Cup matches.

entry.title: Sioux Falls mayoral candidates separated by 2 votes: "Every vote matters"
entry.content: Christine Erickson is two votes ahead of Jamie Smith, the Minnehaha County Auditors and Elections Office said.

entry.title: The $3,000 problem hiding in your kitchen
entry.content: A family of four wastes nearly $3,000 worth of food every year. That figure, released in an April 2025 U.S. Environmental Protection Agency report, nearly doubles the previous federal estimate of $1,500; a number that had been cited for over a decade but was based on 2010 grocery prices.

entry.title: The Long Race to Succeed Jamie Dimon Heats Up
entry.content: Plus, Micron’s stock soars while Apple shares tumble.

entry.title: Daryl Hall says he's 'already starting to feel better' after recent kidney transplant
entry.content: The 79-year-old former Hall & Oates rocker says he recently underwent a kidney transplant from 'a very kind and generous living donor.'

entry.title: Iran strikes ship in Strait of Hormuz in challenge to U.S.-Iran deal
entry.content: Iran attacked a commercial vessel in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday, a U.S. official confirmed, leading a United Nations agency to pause an evacuation effort.

entry.title: Video from inside plane shows crash in Alaskan wilderness
entry.content: A plane carrying 10 people crashed deep in the Alaskan wilderness on Monday. Engine problems forced it down, hundreds of miles from the nearest major city. Kris Van Cleave has new video from inside the plane.

entry.title: Trump administration gives details about vandalism at the Reflecting Pool
entry.content: The Trump administration, for the first time, showed evidence that vandals damaged the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool after the $16 billion renovation project was completed.

entry.title: Bayer wins in Roundup cancer case in the Supreme Court
entry.content: The Supreme Court ruled in favor of Bayer on Thursday in its fight against lawsuits accusing the company of not warning of the cancer risks of the weedkiller Roundup and its chemical glyphosate.

entry.title: New evidence casts doubt on RFK Jr testimony before Senate
entry.content: Kennedy repeatedly said 2019 Samoa trip had ‘nothing to do with vaccines’. An email from his then colleague says they were on a vaccine-related ‘mission’New evidence has emerged that Robert F Kennedy Jr was on a vaccine-related “mission” when he visited Samoa ahead of a deadly measles outbreak in 2019, raising further questions about whether the US health secretary lied to the US Senate when he said the trip had “nothing to do with vaccines”.Records obtained by the Guardian show Kennedy’s colleague told Samoan officials in an email that he and Kennedy were coming as part of a mission to study the island nation’s medical records in the aftermath of a “discontinuity in vaccinations”. Continue reading...

entry.title: NBC News reporter in Venezuela says people she knows might be missing under rubble
entry.content: Ana Vanessa Herrero, reporting for NBC News in Venezuela, sheds light on the hundreds of people looking for loved ones who might be missing under the rubble after two major earthquakes hit near the country's capital on Wednesday.

entry.title: Former California State Parks official charged with videotaping naked male lifeguards in locker room
entry.content: Los Angeles-area prosecutors have charged a former California State Parks official with using hidden cameras to record naked lifeguards in a men's locker room.

entry.title: You may be plugging your Fire TV Stick into the wrong HDMI port
entry.content: It's not so much about using a wrong port, and more so about freeing up the more capable ones for needier devices.

entry.title: India A seize control in Galle: Sudharsan’s masterclass, Jurel-Rasheed stand leave Sri Lanka A chasing shadows
entry.content: India A dominated the opening day of their unofficial Test against Sri Lanka A in Galle, reaching a strong 333/4. Sai Sudharsan led the charge with a brilliant 132, anchoring the innings. Captain Dhruv Jurel (68 not out) and Shaik Rasheed (53 not out) continued the momentum with an unbeaten 116-run partnership, putting India A firmly in command.

entry.title: Rory McIlroy runs into Nick Faldo during Birkdale scouting trip
entry.content: McIlroy, just days removed from his T-32 finish at the U.S. Open at Shinnecock Hills, was at Royal Birkdale in Southport, England, on Thursday ahead of the July 16-19 Open Championship.

entry.title: Former Watford midfielder hit with 11-match ban for racist abuse of team-mate
entry.content: Pierre Dwomoh has been suspended for abusing a team-mate of Indian heritage two months after leaving Watford

entry.title: 2026 Fantasy football profile: 49ers RB Kaelon Black
entry.content: A look at the fantasy profile for 49ers running back Kaelon Black.

entry.title: Bucs' offensive star highlighted as needing an extension
entry.content: There continues to be calls for Baker Mayfield to get an extension, the latest coming from ESPN.

entry.title: What Does the Sale of Progyny Shares by the CFO Mean for Investors?
entry.content: What Does the Sale of Progyny Shares by the CFO Mean for Investors?

entry.title: Apple closed America's first unionized store and blocked workers from transfers — now the union is fighting back
entry.content: Apple closed America's first unionized store and blocked workers from transfers — now the union is fighting back

entry.title: Two Major Indexes Prepare To Add SpaceX As Shares Sink After IPO Surge
entry.content: Two Major Indexes Prepare To Add SpaceX As Shares Sink After IPO Surge

entry.title: Amazon Stock Slips Below Key Level On Rough Day For Cloud Hyperscaler Stocks
entry.content: Amazon Stock Slips Below Key Level On Rough Day For Cloud Hyperscaler Stocks

entry.title: Democratic Socialists topple Harlem incumbent with votes from 7% of registered Dems
entry.content:
A five-term congressman lost his seat Tuesday to a 32-year-old community organizer backed by NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani, and the margin of victory came from just 7% of the district's registered Democrats.
"In some of these districts, nobody votes, it's a very small turnout and the Democratic Socialists of America come in and win," one analyst said.

entry.title: Air Canada flight diverted to Boston after captain suffers medical emergency
entry.content: An Air Canada flight had to be diverted to Boston Wednesday after the captain experienced a medical emergency.

entry.title: UK records its hottest ever June day as health emergencies surge across Europe
entry.content: Temperatures linked to third child’s death in France, where three-quarters of country are under extreme heat alertEurope live – latest updatesThe UK recorded its hottest ever June temperature on Thursday, while brutally hot conditions supercharged by the climate crisis were linked to the death of a third toddler in France and a sharp rise in medical emergencies across Europe.The UK’s new provisional high of 36.4C (97.5F), recorded in Yeovilton, Somerset, surpassed Wednesday’s June record of 36.1C in Gosport, Hampshire, which had beaten the previous peak of 35.6C set in Southampton in 1976. Continue reading...

entry.title: Get a load of this: Humans and great apes share similar giggles
entry.content: A new study suggests humans and great apes have been giggling in similar ways dating back 15 million years

entry.title: Hospitals in England declare critical incidents as machines and IT fail in heat
entry.content: Extreme weather breaks MRI scanners and cooling units, as workload rises for sleep-deprived staff on sweltering wardsDoctors have set out the disastrous impact extreme heat is having on the NHS in England, with radiotherapy machines and MRI scanners failing, critical IT systems stalling and cooling units that serve entire hospitals breaking down.The hot weather has also prompted a surge in admissions and people arriving at A&E, causing severe overcrowding in some places and exacerbating heat-related pressures on infrastructure. Continue reading...

entry.title: From The Sports Desk: Is the Tartan party over?
entry.content: A Brazilian side that featured Neymar sealed the top spot in Group C with a 3-0 win over Scotland yesterday, while the co-hosts Canada progressed to the knockouts for the first time in its history

entry.title: JPMorgan Shakes Up Dimon Succession Race With Two New Presidents
entry.content: JPMorgan Chase named Doug Petno and Troy Rohrbaugh as co-presidents of the company, laying the groundwork to find candidates to succeed chief executive Jamie Dimon.

entry.title: Martin Leibowitz, a Math Whiz Who Transformed Bond Investing, Dies at Age 89
entry.content: As a Salomon Brothers bond researcher, he came up with formulas that still drive financial markets.

entry.title: Supreme Court says U.S. border doesn't include Mexico
entry.content: Migrants who are still in Mexico but near the U.S. border don't enjoy the same rights as those who have already crossed into America, the Supreme Court ruled Thursday in a decision reaffirming the primacy of international boundaries.

entry.title: Wendy's turns lower as meme rally fails to extend to a second day
entry.content: The stock's volatile run appeared largely disconnected from company fundamentals and instead reflected a burst of social media enthusiasm.

entry.title: Supreme Court allows Trump to remove protections from thousands of Haitian and Syrian immigrants
entry.content: WASHINGTON — The Supreme Court on Thursday cleared the way for the Trump administration to remove legal protections from thousands of Haitian and Syrian immigrants in the United States, meaning they could be subject to deportation

entry.title: Supreme Court strikes down Hawaii law curtailing gun carrying on private property
entry.content: The Supreme Court on Thursday sided with gun rights activists in a challenge to Hawaii's law attempting to ban concealed carry permit holders from carrying a firearm on private property -- open to the public -- without written consent from the owner.

entry.title: At This Private-Credit Fund, Exits Have Been Restricted for Four Years and Counting
entry.content: Wealth advisers fear it could be many more years before investors get all their money out of the fund, which invests in consumer and small-business debt.

entry.title: JPMorgan names Doug Petno and Troy Rohrbaugh co-presidents as longtime exec Marianne Lake exits
entry.content: The moves would appear to make Doug Petno and Troy Rohrbaugh the most obvious possible successors to JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon.

entry.title: Belittled, ignored or gaslit – now we know the true cost of not listening to pregnant women | Rhiannon Lucy Cosslett
entry.content: The Ockenden report tells a terrible story of neglect. It’s a story that I – and far too many women I know – recogniseThe findings of Donna Ockenden’s report on maternity services at Nottingham University hospitals NHS trust (NUH) are horrifying. Such is the scale of suffering on the part of mothers, babies and their loved ones that it is almost beyond contemplation. Harrowing details – a room filled with the smell of infection after a woman who was told to labour at home for six days was finally granted surgery; a student doctor being allowed to perform an emergency hysterectomy on a woman, and accidentally removing her bladder; a baby’s remains being disposed of as clinical waste – haunt you long after you finish reading. And then there are all those babies, who should now be exuberant, lovely children, who died because of poor care and neglect.The victims and survivors, who campaigned long and hard for this review, don’t have the luxury of absorbing this information at their own pace, as I had to on Wednesday. They have lived with the brutal reality of it for many long years as they have fought for justice and accountability. These “mad grieving parents” – Sarah Hawkins’ description of how they were made to feel after the death of their daughter Harriet – did not give up in their quest for answers, and though they have been vindicated, I imagine there is a bitter aftertaste. Shamefully, nearly half of the senior members of staff at NUH refused to speak to Ockenden’s review.Rhiannon Lucy Cosslett is a Guardian columnistDo you have an opinion on the issues raised in this article? If you would like to submit a response of up to 300 words by email to be considered for publication in our letters section, please click here. Continue reading...

entry.title: Supreme Court strikes down Hawaii law requiring permission to carry guns in stores and hotels
entry.content: The Supreme Court struck down a Hawaii law requiring people to get permission to carry guns into stores and hotels on Thursday, in its latest opinion backing Second Amendment rights.

entry.title: 'Disruptive' passenger restrained on Jet2 flight dies
entry.content: 'Disruptive' passenger restrained on Jet2 flight dies

entry.title: Three in five young gun violence survivors do not receive mental healthcare – this California bill can fix that
entry.content: The Thrive Act will create a pilot program that offers mental health resources for shooting survivors and witnesses under 25When Marvin Pérez and his family moved from their home country of Guatemala to Oakland, California, he thought it would lead to a better life for them all. But two years ago, when he was 23, Pérez was walking home from a store just four blocks from his family’s house. Someone drove past him and shot at Pérez multiple times, hitting him in his left leg.His physical recovery was difficult. The bullet couldn’t be removed and remains lodged inside the leg. Pérez spent about three months doing physical therapy and resting at home, which meant he couldn’t play soccer, his favorite pastime. The mental toll he endured, however, was far tougher. During the day, all he could think about was the shooting, and when he slept, he had nightmares about what happened to him. It was made even harder by the fact that he felt as if he didn’t have anyone he could share these feelings with. Continue reading...

entry.title: From Currencies to Commodities: Leveraging Gold Ratios in Trading
entry.content: For over 350 years (roughly since 1661 when the first banknotes appeared in Europe), the relationship between gold and paper money has shaped global finance.

entry.title: AI is plowing through the workplace. This new group wants to help people adapt and have jobs
entry.content: A new bipartisan nonprofit wants to help Americans who find they're out of work because of AI

entry.title: Datacentres facing increase in global climate-related legal cases, report finds
entry.content: LSE analysis highlights litigation linked to energy sources, water consumption and air pollutionThe proliferation of datacentres and AI is increasingly at the forefront of environmental litigation around the world from Chile to Ireland, a report has found.In an analysis of about 3,600 climate-related lawsuits filed since 2015, the latest annual review of climate litigation by the London School of Economics (LSE) found a growing number of cases challenging the energy sources, water consumption and air pollution of datacentres, all of which have related climate implications. Continue reading...

entry.title: World Cup schedule today includes USA vs Turkey in California
entry.content: Groups D, E and F conclude group play today as six nations fight to keep their World Cup dreams alive.

entry.title: MLB legend Joe Mauer weighs in on rule changes in sports
entry.content: During his interview with @EdEastonJr, Baseball Hall of Famer Joe Mauer weighed in on rule changes in sports

entry.title: From Queens to Ireland and back, Des Bishop turns a turbulent past into comedy in 'Bridge and Tunnel'
entry.content: Queens-born comic Des Bishop's new "Bridge & Tunnel" special captures his long, winding journey from Irish stardom back to New York, where he finally finds his fast-paced American voice.

entry.title: Türkiye vs USA – Match preview and team news
entry.content: Group D closes on Friday morning at Los Angeles Stadium with Türkiye already eliminated and the USA already through — but Mauricio Pochettino’s side target a third straight win regardless.The USM...

entry.title: USA XI vs Türkiye – Predicted lineup and team news
entry.content: The USA face Türkiye at Los Angeles Stadium in the early hours of Friday morning, already through as Group D winners and targeting an unprecedented third consecutive World Cup victory.Mauricio Pochet...

entry.title: Türkiye vs USA – Predicted lineup and team news
entry.content: Türkiye face the United States at Los Angeles Stadium in the early hours of Friday morning, eliminated from the 2026 World Cup and playing solely to salvage some pride from a deeply disappointing gro...
